question: Will a second named storm form in the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season before July 24, 2026?

factors.description: Climatologically the 2nd Atlantic named storm forms in early August, later than this window (before July 24)
El Nino conditions and below-normal seasonal outlook; ACE running 92% below normal
NHC Tropical Weather Outlook indicates no formation expected next 7 days (covers ~half the window); Saharan dust and persistent high pressure suppressing development
Narrow ~10-day window, effectively only ~5 days live after the 7-day no-formation outlook; development to 40kt named status takes time
Residual possibility a disturbance forms late in the window (July 18-23) that the NHC 7-day outlook cannot yet see

summary: Status-quo NO question with a narrow ~10-day window (formation must be on/before July 23). Multiple convergent suppressive factors stated in the description — El Nino, ACE running 92% below normal, below-normal seasonal outlook, Saharan dust, persistent high pressure — plus an explicit NHC Tropical Weather Outlook indicating no formation expected over the next 7 days (covering ~half the window). Climatologically the 2nd storm forms in early August, later than this window. Only a rapid late-window (July 18-23) homegrown development, which the current 7-day outlook cannot yet see, could flip this to YES — low probability against a hostile environment. Final estimate ~6%.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 6% (NO)**

This resolves whether a second named storm (Bertha) forms before July 24, 2026. All signals point to NO:

- **Suppressive regime**: El Niño, ACE running 92% below normal, below-normal seasonal outlook, Saharan dust, and persistent high pressure across the basin.
- **NHC 7-day outlook**: explicitly indicated no tropical formation expected — covering roughly half the remaining window.
- **Climatology**: the second Atlantic named storm typically forms in early August, later than this window, and skews even later in suppressed seasons.
- **Narrow window**: only ~10 days remain, and tropical development to 40kt naming intensity takes time.

The only realistic path to YES is a rapid, homegrown Gulf/Caribbean system spinning up in the final ~5 days that the current outlook can't yet see — a low-probability event in this hostile environment. Final probability: **0.06**.
